14.description: No standing — standing or parking prohibited at all times
defenses: Sign was missing, obscured, or illegible at the time of the violation
Vehicle was actively loading or unloading passengers
Emergency circumstances required the stop
No Standing sign placement did not conform to NYC DOT specifications
Vehicle qualifies as an authorized vehicle under the posted restriction
Sign was missing, obscured, or illegible
Permit or placard visible and valid at time of issuance
Sign placement did not conform to NYC DOT specifications
Snow emergency was not officially declared at the time of the violation
Vehicle was moved before enforcement began
Sign indicating snow emergency route was missing or obscured
Vehicle is exempt under snow emergency rules
Bus stop sign was missing, obscured, or illegible
Vehicle was actively loading or unloading passengers (momentary stop)
Bus stop markings were absent or faded beyond recognition
Sign placement did not conform to NYC DOT specifications

20.description: No parking — specific days/times as posted
defenses: Sign was missing, obscured, or illegible at the time of the violation
Violation was issued outside the restricted hours or days
Conflicting signs created ambiguity about the restriction
Sign placement did not conform to NYC DOT specifications
Sign was missing, obscured, or illegible
Street cleaning did not occur on that day (e.g., holiday suspension)
Alternate side parking was officially suspended on that date
Vehicle was moved before the street cleaner passed
Conflicting signs created genuine ambiguity
Meter was broken or malfunctioning at the time of parking
Receipt or payment confirmation shows time had not yet expired
Meter displayed an error and could not accept payment
Payment was made via ParkNYC app and confirmation exists

38.description: No parking — no muni-meter receipt displayed
defenses: Receipt was purchased and displayed but may have fallen or been displaced
Muni-meter was out of service at the time
ParkNYC app payment was active and can be confirmed
Receipt was valid and placed on dashboard per instructions

40.description: No parking — within 15 feet of a fire hydrant
defenses: Vehicle was parked more than 15 feet from the hydrant (measurement dispute)
Hydrant was a private hydrant not subject to the restriction
Hydrant location was not clearly visible or marked
Vehicle was actively attended and would have moved immediately upon request
Vehicle was actively loading or unloading commercial goods
No legal parking was available within a reasonable distance
Vehicle was attended and prepared to move immediately
Location is outside a commercial district where brief double parking is tolerated
Vehicle was not located within the defined midtown core boundaries
Active loading or unloading of commercial goods was in progress
Vehicle was attended and immediately ready to move
Posted signage defining the zone was missing or illegible
No bike lane markings or signage were present at that location
Vehicle was actively loading or unloading
Bike lane was temporarily closed or obstructed by construction
Vehicle was a authorized emergency or service vehicle

69.description: Failure to display a registration sticker
defenses: Registration was current; sticker was lost, stolen, or delayed in mail
Vehicle was recently registered and sticker had not yet been received
Registration renewal documentation can be produced showing compliance
Sticker was affixed but may have been obscured at time of inspection
Registration was renewed prior to or on the date of the violation
Renewal was pending due to a DMV processing delay
Documentation shows renewal was submitted before the expiration date
Vehicle is registered out of state and was compliant under that state's rules
Inspection was completed prior to or on the date of the violation
Inspection appointment was scheduled and vehicle was in compliance process
Sticker was affixed but officer may have misread the expiration date
Vehicle qualifies for an exemption from periodic inspection requirements

78.description: Nighttime parking of a commercial vehicle on a residential street
defenses: Vehicle does not meet the definition of a commercial vehicle under NYC Admin Code
Street is not a designated residential street under the applicable rules
Parking occurred outside the prohibited nighttime hours
Vehicle displays proper permits allowing overnight residential parking
